generated_comment:
Mohammed: Arre, isme bura kya hai? Jo jis kaam mein better ho wahi sambhale, practical baat hai. Mard bahar ke kaam, finance aur driving dekh leta hai, aur aurat ghar ka system, khana, bachche naturally zyada achhe se sambhalti hai.
Wife: Haan, aajkal log har cheez ko issue bana dete hain.
Mohammed: Bas, kabhi kabhi husband kitchen mein chai bana de toh log bolte hain wah kitna modern hai, lekin asli zimmedari toh ghar ki aurat par hi achhi lagti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as merely practical, assigning finances and outside work to men and domestic management to women. It encodes efficiency framing, 'he handles finances, she handles home,' and celebrates minimal male domestic contribution as exceptional.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 48. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 26 year old daughter Sana has told you and your wife that she and her husband have decided to wait at least three more years before having children because they both want to establish their careers first. You are sitting with your wife after Sana has left. You are telling your wife what you think of this decision and what it means about the kind of wife Sana is and what Islam says about a woman's role in marriage and why you think Sana's husband is not guiding her correctly.
